preamble: Retroflexion of the dental nasal [n] to the retroflex nasal [ɳ], a process known as 'Nati' in Sanskrit grammar. This typically occurs when a dental nasal is preceded by a retroflex consonant (like [ʂ], [r], or [ɽ]) within the same word, even across other vowels or consonants (except dental/palatal ones).

examples.note: Nominative vs Instrumental case; [n] becomes [ɳ] due to preceding [r] in the root.
General retroflexion of dental nasals in various Middle Indo-Aryan dialects.

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Fortson, B. W. (2010). Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction.
